reasoning: Merged 22 input patches covering spreadsheet manipulation failures and successes. Identified 8 major theme clusters: (1) tool usage/execution hygiene, (2) formula construction, (3) library choice/object types, (4) data validation/bounds, (5) row operation safety, (6) output range preservation, (7) verification workflows, (8) task classification. Applied prevalence bias - patterns appearing in 3+ patches (formula string validation, pandas/openpyxl distinction, reverse row deletion, post-save verification, header protection) prioritized. Resolved conflicts: clarified pandas for data processing vs openpyxl for structural changes; confirmed openpyxl for formula assignment. Deduplicated overlapping guidance on code block purity and empty value handling into unified sections.

memory_items.title: Task Type Classification
memory_items.lesson: Advisory queries asking 'what formula' require text/markedown explanations only; do NOT execute as Python code cells
memory_items.generalization: Before code execution, determine if task needs file modification (requires pandas/openpyxl) versus formula guidance (text explanation only)

memory_items.title: Code Block Purity
memory_items.lesson: Execute_code tool parameters must contain ONLY valid Python code without prose, tool markers, or XML-style tags
memory_items.generalization: Separate explanatory commentary completely from executable code strings to prevent syntax parsing failures

memory_items.title: Pandas vs OpenPyXL Selection
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for bulk data computations; use openpyxl for structural changes, formula assignment, or formatting preservation
memory_items.generalization: When cell-level precision or workbook structure integrity matters, load/save via openpyxl Workbook objects rather than DataFrame conversions

memory_items.title: Worksheet Object Limitations
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl Worksheet objects lack pandas DataFrame attributes like .shape, .columns, .head()
memory_items.generalization: Use pandas.read_excel() for shape/column verification; use ws.max_row/max_column for openpyxl-native queries

memory_items.title: Dynamic Range Detection
memory_items.lesson: Always derive iteration bounds from runtime properties (ws.max_row, len(df)) instead of hardcoded row numbers
memory_items.generalization: Validate actual data extent before loops to prevent IndexError and boundary misalignment

memory_items.title: Index Convention Consistency
memory_items.lesson: pandas uses 0-indexed rows while Excel uses 1-indexed; apply +/-1 offsets uniformly at every cross-tool access point
memory_items.generalization: Document and consistently apply index conversions when mixing pandas DataFrames with openpyxl Worksheet coordinates

memory_items.title: Reverse Row Deletion Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Collect row indices first, sort descending, then delete to prevent index shifting during multi-row removal
memory_items.generalization: Buffer all targets before deletion operations; iterate from highest to lowest index to maintain alignment integrity

memory_items.title: Empty Value Handling
memory_items.lesson: Check both pd.isna() AND isinstance(str, '') since empty strings pass notna() checks
memory_items.generalization: Include explicit empty-string validation alongside NaN/None checks when evaluating cell usability

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Task Classification Before Execution
- **Advisory Queries**: Instructions asking "what formula" or "how to" without file modification requests require plain text/markdown responses only
- **File Operations**: Only invoke Python code cells when actual workbook loading/modification is required
- **Mixed Tasks**: Explain first in text, then execute file operations in separate code cells

### Code Execution Hygiene
- **Pure Executable Content**: execute_code parameters must contain ONLY valid Python syntax without natural language, tool markers, or markup artifacts
- **Syntax Validation**: Parser-level line 1 errors often indicate wrapper/formatting issues rather than logic bugs
- **Debug Loop Recognition**: Identical consecutive errors suggest structural problems requiring format audit, not code modification

### Library Selection Strategy
| Operation | Recommended Tool | Rationale |
|-----------|------------------|----------|
| Bulk data computation | pandas | Efficient columnar operations |
| Structural changes (row/col delete/insert) | openpyxl | Preserves workbook structure |
| Formula creation/assignment | openpyxl | Maintains formula strings |
| Formatting preservation | openpyxl | Cell attributes retained |
| Shape/column verification | pandas.read_excel() | DataFrame has .shape/.columns |

### Worksheet Object Awareness
- `openpyxl.load_workbook()` returns Workbook containing Worksheet objects (no `.shape` attribute)
- Use `ws.max_row`, `ws.max_column` for dimension queries on Worksheet objects
- Convert to DataFrame via `pd.read_excel()` for aggregation/statistical analysis

### Data Validation Best Practices
- **Header Verification**: Print column names and sample rows after loading before processing
- **Constraint Validation**: Verify referenced cells contain expected numeric values before arithmetic operations
- **Empty State Checks**: Test `value is None OR pd.isna(value) OR (isinstance(value, str) and not value.strip())`
- **Formula Protection**: Check `cell.data_type == 'f'` before overwriting to preserve calculation chains

### Row Operation Safety Protocol
1. Collect all target row indices into a list during forward scan
2. Sort collected indices in descending order
3. Execute deletions via `ws.delete_rows()` starting from highest index
4. Include null-safe guards before string method calls on cell values

### Cross-Sheet Copying Precautions
- Never overwrite destination header row unless explicitly instructed
- Start data writes at row 2 (or N+1 where N=header rows) regardless of source position
- Spot-check header integrity after any cross-sheet transfer
- Account for merged cell migration when recreating sheets

### Formula Construction Guidelines
- **String Validation**: Ensure generated formulas have exactly one leading `=` sign
- **Range Logic**: Construct row-aware ranges for per-cell calculations; avoid static absolute ranges across multiple output rows
- **Circular References**: Exclude target cell from its own aggregate formula range (split into comma-separated sub-ranges)
- **Date Boundaries**: Calculate month-end via first-day-of-next-month minus one day; never hardcode day 31
- **Multi-Criteria Matching**: Use `SUMPRODUCT+ISNUMBER(MATCH(...))` pattern for array criteria; `SUMIFS` accepts only single-value criteria

### Date and Text Processing
- **Case Sensitivity**: Excel performs case-insensitive comparisons; Python requires explicit normalization (.lower()/casefold())
- **Data Type Verification**: Always check `isinstance(cell.value, datetime.datetime)` before string-based date parsing
- **Number Format Behavior**: `number_format` controls display only; underlying value type remains unchanged

### Post-Modification Verification Workflow
1. Save workbook, then reload via fresh Workbook object instance
2. Spot-check critical cells for correct value AND formatting persistence
3. Log transformation metrics (rows processed, duplicates found, cells modified)
4. Compare pre/post states for reference/metadata row integrity

### Workbook Lifecycle Management
- Create output directories before saving (`os.makedirs(exist_ok=True)`)
- Prefer temporary-file workflow: save to `.tmp` path, validate success, rename to final location
- Call `wb.close()` after saving to release file locks
- Explicitly check sheet existence before modification or deletion operations

changelog_entries: Consolidated 22 trace patches covering spreadsheet manipulation best practices
Added task classification section distinguishing advisory vs file operation queries
Unified code execution hygiene guidelines addressing SyntaxError patterns
Created library selection decision table for pandas/openpyxl usage contexts
Integrated row deletion safety protocol from multiple successful trajectories
Combined formula construction guidance including circular reference prevention
Standardized verification workflow section with post-save reloading requirement
Merged empty value handling patterns across openpyxl/pandas contexts
